mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.3pp threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or flat 60-day forward return in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over 4 years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and boosts equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day returns during supply chain normalization periods, or the predictive power (Rank IC) drops below 0.01 when filtered to this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 235 while the federal funds rate stays below 0.5 percent, markets anticipate aggressive future tightening and stagflation risks, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold condition produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales reveals underlying demand momentum; when both series trend upward together, it confirms sustainable consumption growth that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The co-movement signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction and manufacturing weakness, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following the 1.5% INDPRO decline threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.
